meaning: n.[植]月桂树,(pl.)桂冠,殊荣vt.使戴桂冠,授予荣誉
examples: Its huge columns were wreathed with laurel and magnolia.
She led him round the laurel path and to the walk where the ivy grew so thickly.
Stan Laurel was born at number 3, Argyll Street.
Today, the house is a museum and sits virtually hidden in the windy Laurel Highlands of western Pennsylvania.
Ian Payne, the chief executive of Laurel, said it was "a great transaction for both parties".
